text.title: AI Diff Size Discipline Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every ai-diff-size-discipline artefact must obey. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: LOC touched (excluding tests + generated code) MUST be <= 200. Diffs above the cap require a split-plan before merge.
rule.rationale: Reviewer attention drops above ~200 LOC; bugs hide in the middle of large diffs.

rule.statement: Files touched MUST be <= 8 per PR. Above the cap, refactor and feature must be split.
rule.rationale: Cross-file edits compound review cost super-linearly; the cap forces decomposition.

rule.statement: PR MUST contain exactly one semantic unit (1 feature OR 1 refactor OR 1 fix), never two.
rule.rationale: Mixed units make rollback impossible; reverting a bad feature also reverts an unrelated refactor.

rule.statement: When any cap is breached, the rubric MUST emit a split-plan with ordered slices and per-slice acceptance criteria.
rule.rationale: Without a plan, the author tries to negotiate exceptions; the plan reframes the conversation around shipping the same work in smaller PRs.

rule.statement: Cap overrides MUST be approved by a named reviewer + a one-line justification in the PR. Self-approval is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without a gate, caps decay into suggestions and the discipline collapses.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers (e.g. generated-code dump, lockfile-only diff, pure docs reorg, emergency hotfix), do NOT score the diff with this rubric. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip and route the PR to its native review track.
rule.rationale: Mis-applying the cap rubric to migrations or codegen produces noise that erodes trust in the gate; an explicit skip-record makes non-application auditable.
